name: HTMX
description: Access AJAX, CSS Transitions, WebSockets and Server Sent Events directly in HTML using attributes

description.value: Access AJAX, CSS Transitions, WebSockets and Server Sent Events directly in HTML using attributes

license.comment: Zero-Clause BSD; changed from 2-clause BSD in January 2024

initial-release.comment: v1.0.0 released November 24, 2020; successor to intercooler.js (2013)

github-stars.comment: ~46.9k stars as of December 2025; was #1 JavaScript Rising Stars in January 2025

active-maintenance.comment: Actively maintained with 445+ contributors; 3,485 commits; v4.0 planned for 2026

reactivity-model.comment: HTMX uses a fundamentally different paradigm - server returns HTML fragments; no client-side reactivity model

component-model.comment: HTML attributes (hx-get, hx-post, etc.) define behavior; no JavaScript components

rendering-paradigm.comment: Declarative HTML attributes define AJAX behavior

state-management-approach.comment: Server is the single source of truth; no client-side state management - fundamentally different paradigm

opinionatedness.comment: Opinionated about hypermedia-driven approach; server returns HTML, not JSON

backward-compatibility.comment: Strong commitment: 'htmx written in 2025 will look similar to htmx in 2035'

compiles-to.comment: Pure JavaScript runtime; no compilation required

typescript-support.comment: No TypeScript definitions in core; HTML attribute-based so limited TS benefits

template-syntax.comment: HTML attributes (hx-get, hx-post, hx-trigger, hx-swap, etc.)

styling-approach.comment: No built-in styling; supports CSS transitions via hx-swap transitions

build-required.comment: No build step; single CDN script tag or npm install

reactive-primitives.comment: No client-side reactive primitives; server provides state via HTML

computed-values.comment: No client-side computed values; server computes and returns HTML

two-way-binding.comment: No two-way binding; forms submit to server and receive HTML responses

event-handling.comment: hx-trigger attribute handles events to trigger AJAX requests

conditional-rendering.comment: No client-side conditional rendering; server sends appropriate HTML

list-rendering.comment: No client-side list rendering; server returns rendered HTML lists

component-slots.comment: No component system; works with existing HTML structure

teleport-portal.comment: hx-target and hx-swap-oob allow content to be placed anywhere in DOM

suspense.comment: hx-indicator shows loading states during AJAX requests

error-boundaries.comment: No error boundaries; handle via htmx:responseError event

context-provide-inject.comment: No client-side context system; server maintains all state

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: No refs; htmx events provide DOM context when needed

official-router.comment: No client-side router; uses server-side routing with hx-boost for SPA-like navigation

router-name.comment: No router; hx-boost enhances standard links for AJAX navigation

lazy-loading-routes.comment: No client-side routing; content loaded via AJAX on demand

scroll-restoration.comment: hx-push-url with history API provides scroll restoration

built-in-state.comment: Server is single source of truth; no client-side state management

built-in-global-state.comment: No client-side global state; server maintains all state

global-state-library.comment: No client-side state library needed; server is state store

devtools-state-inspection.comment: No client-side state to inspect; use network tab and server-side debugging

state-persistence.comment: Not applicable; server handles state persistence

official-cli.comment: No CLI needed; single script include

hot-module-replacement.comment: Not applicable; no build step typically used

browser-devtools.comment: No official browser extension; use network tab and htmx:* events

vs-code-extension.comment: Multiple VS Code extensions: htmx-tags, HTMX IntelliSense, HTMX Attributes

error-messages.comment: Clear event-based error handling; htmx:responseError provides context

documentation-quality.comment: Good documentation with examples, essays, and reference; active community support

interactive-tutorial.comment: No official interactive tutorial; examples in docs

playground.comment: No official playground; requires server for meaningful demos

bundle-size-full.comment: ~18 kB with common extensions; extensions now versioned separately

virtual-dom.comment: No virtual DOM; directly swaps HTML content from server responses

tree-shaking.comment: Single library; extensions are separate packages

code-splitting.comment: No client-side code splitting; server returns HTML fragments on demand

lazy-components.comment: No component system; HTML loaded via AJAX on demand

ssr-capable.comment: Designed for server-rendered HTML; SSR is the default paradigm, not an add-on

streaming-ssr.comment: Receives complete HTML fragments; streaming support via SSE extension

partial-hydration.comment: No hydration needed; server returns ready-to-use HTML

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Not included in js-framework-benchmark; fundamentally different paradigm (server-rendered HTML)

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: ~350k weekly downloads (htmx.org package); note: old broken 'htmx' package exists

component-libraries.comment: No UI component libraries; uses server-rendered HTML with any backend

meta-framework.comment: Works with any server framework (Django, Flask, Laravel, Rails, Go, etc.)

major-companies-using.value: Smaller companies, internal tools, content management systems
major-companies-using.comment: Growing adoption; real-world port showed 96% reduction in JS dependencies

official-sponsor-backer.comment: Created by Carson Gross at Big Sky Software; GitHub Accelerator 2023 cohort

discord-community-size.comment: Active Discord at htmx.org/discord; exact count not verified

stack-overflow-questions.comment: Questions tagged with 'htmx' on Stack Overflow; exact count not verified

job-market-demand.comment: Growing; 40% YoY increase in adoption for internal tools; niche but expanding

official-testing-library.comment: No official testing library; test server endpoints as normal

testing-library-name.comment: Use standard server-side testing + E2E tools

component-testing.comment: No components to test; test server templates and endpoints

e2e-testing-integration.comment: Works with Playwright, Cypress, etc. for E2E testing

snapshot-testing.comment: Test server-rendered HTML snapshots on backend

mobile-framework.comment: No mobile framework; works in WebViews

pwa-support.comment: No built-in PWA support; requires server connection

desktop-framework.comment: No desktop framework; works in Electron/Tauri WebViews

learning-curve.comment: Very gentle; familiar HTML attributes, minimal concepts, no build step

concepts-to-learn.comment: hx-* attributes, targets/swaps, and triggers; hypermedia concepts help

time-to-hello-world.comment: ~1 minute; add CDN script and hx-get attribute

migration-path.comment: v1.x to v2.x migration guide; real-world React-to-HTMX port documented

incremental-adoption.comment: Designed for incremental adoption; add to any page without changes
